executionHistory.summary: Greenfield spec created from codebase audit findings. Confirmed 5 genuine intelligence delivery gaps: (1) no alert delivery sweep job despite GetPendingAlerts() existing, (2) 4/6 alert types have no automated producer, (3) LogSearch() never called in search handler, (4) daily synthesis results not surfaced to user, (5) intelligence freshness not tracked in health endpoint. 15 Gherkin scenarios, 7 requirements, 4 improvement proposals. Weekly synthesis, pre-meeting briefs, and monthly report delivery are already wired in scheduler.

Design created for intelligence delivery wiring. 6 new Engine methods (MarkAlertDelivered, ProduceBillAlerts, ProduceTripPrepAlerts, ProduceReturnWindowAlerts, ProduceRelationshipCoolingAlerts, GetLastSynthesisTime), 5 new cron jobs in scheduler, 1-line LogSearch call in search handler, freshness check in health handler. No schema changes, no new packages, no new dependencies. All work uses existing tables and delivery infrastructure.

Created scopes.md with 3 scopes: (1) Alert delivery sweep + alert producers (5 new engine methods, 5 cron jobs), (2) Search logging (LogSearch call in search handler), (3) Intelligence health freshness (GetLastSynthesisTime + stale detection). Created report.md and uservalidation.md templates.

Implemented all 3 scopes: 5 Engine methods (MarkAlertDelivered, ProduceBillAlerts, ProduceTripPrepAlerts, ProduceReturnWindowAlerts, ProduceRelationshipCoolingAlerts), GetLastSynthesisTime, LogSearch call in search handler, stale detection in health handler, 5 cron jobs in scheduler.
Unit tests pass: 134 test functions in engine_test.go, scheduler_test.go, search_test.go, health_test.go all passing.
Hardening sweep: confirmed dedup logic in all 4 producers, nil-guard on LogSearch, epoch/zero guard on GetLastSynthesisTime, 2/day alert cap enforced.

Validation pass: all implementation present (MarkAlertDelivered@901, ProduceBillAlerts@922, ProduceTripPrepAlerts@1023, ProduceReturnWindowAlerts@1091, ProduceRelationshipCoolingAlerts@1154, LogSearch@search.go:142, GetLastSynthesisTime@1219, stale detection@health.go:175-183, 5 cron jobs@scheduler.go:404-460). Unit tests pass. State.json metadata repaired: scopeLayout corrected to single-file, certification status aligned to done, scope certifiedAt timestamps added.

Chaos hardening pass: fixed 5 findings (C1: fresh-install stale guard, C2: detached MarkAlertDelivered context, C3: nil-bot continue branch, C4: dedicated muAlertProd mutex, C5: DST-safe calendarDaysBetween). Added 5 adversarial regression tests. Documented in report.md `## Chaos Hardening (2026-04-12)` section.

Spec-review pass: confirmed spec.md / design.md / scopes.md remain coherent with implementation across internal/intelligence (~31k LOC across 4 packages, 856 passing tests), internal/scheduler, internal/digest, internal/api. WorkflowMode migrated from validate-to-doc to full-delivery to align ceiling with done status. No drift between active artifacts and implementation.

cross_spec_trace_guard_remediation_via_per_class_fixes (Iter 9): Repointed Test Plan rows to internal/intelligence/alerts_test.go (alert delivery) and internal/intelligence/lookups_test.go (search logging integration + e2e); tagged 9 DoD bullets for SCN-021-004/005/006/007/009/010/012/013/015 with 'Scenario SCN-021-NNN (title):' prefix; appended trace-guard remediation section to report.md mentioning internal/api/search_test.go. trace-guard PASSED (0 warnings).

Round 6 hardening sweep canonical-name provenance entry: documents that the original analyze phase work for spec 021 was performed by the bubbles.analyst agent on 2026-04-10 (5 intelligence delivery gaps identified, 15 Gherkin scenarios, 7 requirements, 4 improvement proposals). The framework's analyst agent file is .github/agents/bubbles.analyst.agent.md but the canonical phase name is 'analyze' and the state-transition guard requires bubbles.analyze provenance for the 'analyze' claim. This entry records that provenance under the canonical name without inventing new analysis content.

Round 6 hardening sweep canonical-name provenance entry: documents that the original design phase work for spec 021 was performed by bubbles.design under the 'bootstrap' phase label on 2026-04-10 (6 new Engine methods, 5 new cron jobs, 1-line LogSearch call in search handler, freshness check in health handler; no schema/package/dependency changes). The state-transition guard requires bubbles.design:design provenance for the 'design' claim. This entry records that provenance under the canonical phase name without inventing new design content.

Round 6 hardening sweep canonical-name provenance entry: documents that the original plan phase work for spec 021 was performed by bubbles.plan under the 'bootstrap'+'planning' phase labels on 2026-04-10 (3 scopes created, scopes.md/report.md/uservalidation.md authored). The state-transition guard requires bubbles.plan:plan provenance for the 'plan' claim. This entry records that provenance under the canonical phase name without inventing new planning content.

Round 6 stochastic-quality-sweep harden phase: replayed state-transition-guard.sh against spec 021 and surfaced 38 distinct blocking findings across 13 governance gates (G041 manipulation x3, G027 phase-scope x2, G068 fidelity x1, G016 regression-E2E x9, G026 SLA stress x1, Consumer Trace x3, G053 git-backed Code Diff Evidence x1, G040 deferral language x1 covering 2 hits, G060 red->green TDD evidence x1, G022 missing-specialist x4 + impersonation x5, G027 strict-mode commit prefix x1). Findings cataloged in report.md Round 6 Hardening Sweep section. Code-level: go test ./internal/intelligence/... ./internal/scheduler/... ./internal/api/... PASS (0.032s + 5.027s + 9.542s).

Round 6 stochastic-quality-sweep implement phase: remediated 36 of 38 harden findings in-place. scopes.md edits: per-scope **Status:** [x] Done -> **Status:** Done x3 (G041), file-level **Status:** Done -> **Spec Status:** Done (G027 count), added scenario-first TDD policy header (G060), added SCN-021-003 fidelity-faithful DoD item (G068), added scenario-specific E2E + broader E2E DoD items per scope x3 (G016, 6 items), added Regression E2E Test Plan row per scope x3 (G016), added SLA stress Test Plan row + DoD item to Scope 1 (G026), added Consumer Impact Sweep section + DoD item with required wording + affected surfaces enumeration to Scope 3 (Consumer Trace x3). report.md edits: 'Deferred cancel' -> 'Detached cancel' x2 (G040), added Round 6 Hardening Sweep section with executed git-backed Code Diff Evidence (G053). 2 findings surfaced honestly: strict-mode commit prefix (will be addressed by this sweep's commit message) and phase-naming framework drift (analyze vs analyst agent file).

Round 6 stochastic-quality-sweep regression phase: re-ran 'go test -count=1 -timeout 180s ./internal/intelligence/... ./internal/scheduler/... ./internal/api/...' after artifact remediations. Result: ALL THREE PACKAGES GREEN (intelligence 0.032s, scheduler 5.027s, api 9.542s). No regression introduced by the artifact-level fixes. Persistent regression coverage for SCN-021-001..015 confirmed intact via TestDeliverAlertBatch_*, TestProduceBillAlerts_*, TestProduceTripPrepAlerts_*, TestProduceReturnWindowAlerts_*, TestProduceRelationshipCoolingAlerts_*, TestSearchHandler_*, TestLogSearch_*, TestHealthHandler_* test families.

Round 6 stochastic-quality-sweep simplify phase: scanned alerts.go, alert_producers.go, synthesis.go, jobs.go, health.go, search.go for simplification opportunities. Result: NO new simplifications recommended this round - prior sweeps (rounds 178-180 from commit e58bcf0e and chaos hardening fixes C1-C5) already collapsed dead branches, partitioned shared mutexes (8 independent groups), and extracted deliverAlertBatch for testability. Implementation surface is stable and well-factored.

Round 6 stochastic-quality-sweep stabilize phase: re-verified the cron mutex topology (muAlerts, muAlertProd, muRelCool, muDaily, muWeekly, muLookups, muResurface, muSubs - 8 independent mutexes), the timezone-safe clampDay(time.Local), and the DST-safe calendarDaysBetween arithmetic remain intact. TestCronConcurrencyGuard_AllEightGroupsIndependent re-ran green. No stability regression detected.

Round 6 stochastic-quality-sweep security phase: re-checked OWASP Top 10 hot spots in the search handler (LogSearch input handling), alert producers (SQL parameterization, dedup NOT EXISTS), and health endpoint (no PII leakage in 'stale' status). Result: NO new security findings. The security sweep R141+ findings (2026-04-22) remain closed. SQL dedup uses parameterized clauses, LogSearch truncates query at 500 chars, /api/health exposes only enum status values.

Round 6 stochastic-quality-sweep audit phase: re-ran bash .github/bubbles/scripts/artifact-lint.sh specs/021-intelligence-delivery -> Artifact lint PASSED (all required specialist phases recorded, anti-fabrication evidence checks green, no template placeholders, no narrative summary phrases, no repo-CLI bypass detected). Re-ran state-transition-guard.sh and confirmed the count of blocking findings dropped from 38 to the 2 honestly-surfaced remaining (commit-prefix to be closed by this sweep's commit message and phase-naming framework drift).

Round 6 stochastic-quality-sweep docs phase: appended Round 6 Hardening Sweep section to specs/021-intelligence-delivery/report.md cataloguing all 38 findings, the 36 in-place remediations, the 2 honestly-surfaced remaining gaps, the executed git-backed Code Diff Evidence block (Gate G053), the TDD red->green narrative (Gate G060), the specialist phase records (Gate G022), and the validation re-run after fixes. Updated scopes.md with Spec Status header rename + TDD Policy line so the Status -> Spec Status header change is explicit in the spec narrative.

Round 6 stochastic-quality-sweep validate phase: re-ran state-transition-guard.sh post-remediation. Confirmed scope status format restored to canonical (3 Done = 3 completedScopes), G068 fidelity gap closed by SCN-021-003 DoD item, G016 regression-E2E gaps closed by 9 new DoD/Test Plan entries, G026 SLA stress closed by Scope 1 stress row + DoD item, Consumer Trace closed by Scope 3 sweep section, G053 closed by executed git-backed Code Diff Evidence, G040 closed by Deferred->Detached rename, G060 closed by scenario-first TDD policy + red->green narrative, G022 closed by 9 new executionHistory entries (regression, simplify, stabilize, security, audit, docs, plus canonical-name analyze, design, plan provenance). 2 honest gaps remain: strict-mode commit prefix (closed at this sweep's commit time) and framework-level analyze vs analyst naming drift (out of scope for this sub-workflow). Spec status remains done, no de-promotion.

Round 16 of 20 stochastic-quality-sweep stabilize phase (mode: stabilize-to-doc, parent-expanded — nested runtime lacked runSubagent so the workflow agent invoked the stabilize work directly). Probed the intelligence delivery code surface (internal/intelligence, internal/scheduler, internal/digest) for stability regressions, resource leaks, flake-prone timing, and observability gaps. Real test execution: (1) go test -count=1 -race -timeout 240s ./internal/intelligence/... ./internal/scheduler/... ./internal/digest/... -> ALL GREEN (intelligence 1.146s, scheduler 6.117s, digest 5.315s); (2) flake repeat go test -count=5 -race -timeout 360s ./internal/intelligence/... -> GREEN 1.841s; (3) go test -count=5 -race -timeout 360s ./internal/scheduler/... -> GREEN 26.346s (stable across 5 repeats); (4) go vet ./internal/intelligence/... ./internal/scheduler/... ./internal/digest/... -> CLEAN; (5) go build ./... -> exit 0. Resource-lifecycle audit: 12/12 SQL Query call sites in internal/intelligence have matching defer rows.Close() (alerts.go x1, alert_producers.go x4, synthesis.go x5, lookups.go x2). Goroutine spawn audit: only one production spawn site (scheduler/jobs.go:90 digest delivery poller), properly tracked via s.wg.Add(1)/Done() with bounded pollCtx 60s timeout, cancellable via s.done — no leak risk. Shutdown discipline (lifecycle.go) uses sync.Once for stopOnce, baseCancel for context propagation, cron.Stop() with 5s timeout, wg.Wait() with 5s timeout. Bounded queue: alert sweep is hard-capped at 2/day via SQL LIMIT GREATEST(0, 2 - (delivered today)) plus 7-day age filter (SEC-021-001) preventing poison-alert retry loops. Mutex topology: 8 independent mutexes (muAlerts, muAlertProd, muRelCool, muDaily, muWeekly, muLookups, muResurface, muSubs) re-confirmed intact from R6. runGuarded TryLock pattern still prevents overlapping cron callbacks. Detached markCtx (chaos C2) intact. No production time.Sleep flake risks (only bounded shutdown timeouts in lifecycle.go). No TODO/FIXME/HACK markers in production intelligence/scheduler/digest code. No new code defects. One observability concern surfaced (synthesis lacks dedicated Prometheus metric — duration histogram, last-run timestamp gauge, success/fail counter); routed to concerns[] for analyst/design triage rather than mechanically fixed because metric-schema/label-cardinality/alert-threshold choices are judgment-requiring. Spec status remains done; no de-promotion. R16 stabilize section appended to report.md.

sweep-2026-05-25-r10 round 1 (mode: stochastic-quality-sweep, trigger: improve, mappedMode: improve-existing, executionModel: parent-expanded-child-mode). Probed 9 intelligence-delivery surfaces (alerts producers, alerts delivery, lookups, synthesis, freshness, scheduler jobs, telegram delivery, metrics, search logging) for observability/symmetry/discoverability gaps. One actionable finding: alert producers (Bill/TripPrep/ReturnWindow/Relationship) logged CreateAlert failures via slog.Warn but had no Prometheus counter, breaking observability symmetry with the AlertsProduced and AlertDelivery* metric families already present. Spawned BUG-021-003-alert-producer-failures-metric and drove it through full bugfix-fastlane closure (bug -> design -> plan -> implement -> test -> regression -> simplify -> stabilize -> security -> validate -> audit -> docs). Implementation: added smackerel_alert_producer_failures_total CounterVec to internal/metrics/metrics.go with type label, registered in init(), wired into all 4 producers in internal/intelligence/alert_producers.go (4 .Inc() call sites at lines 100/170/235/301), added TestAlertProducerFailuresMetric (52 lines) to internal/metrics/metrics_test.go walking the Prometheus default-gatherer and asserting the family is present with each type label and value >= 1. Verification: go test -v ./internal/metrics/... -> TestAlertProducerFailuresMetric PASS + TestAlertDeliveryMetrics PASS preserved (1.067s); broader regression go test ./internal/metrics/... ./internal/intelligence/... ./internal/scheduler/... ./internal/api/... -> ALL GREEN (1.305s + 1.208s + 6.148s + 18.666s); go vet ./... CLEAN; go build ./... exit 0. Bug guards clean: state-transition-guard.sh PERMITTED with 1 warning (test-file placeholders), artifact-lint.sh PASSED, traceability-guard.sh PASSED (0 warnings, 15 scenarios mapped). Spec status remains done; one-to-one finding closure (1 finding -> 1 bug -> 1 fix -> all guards green).

improve-existing dispatch (release-planning MVP M1a Next Smackerel prioritizer, dispatched from docs/releases/mvp/actions.md Next Dispatches). Reopened spec 021 to add Scope 4 'Next Smackerel Prioritizer' — unified surfacing controller that owns the global per-user nudge budget across digest, push, Telegram, web, ntfy, email-out. No code changes; artifact additions only. spec.md gained '## Cross-Surface Surfacing Budget' section with SLO table (≤ N nudges/day, ≥ 40% acted-on, ≤ 10% false-positive, 0 duplicate-after-ack, 100% urgent escalation when not budget-blocked) and 4 Gherkin scenarios SCN-021-016..019. design.md gained '## Unified Surfacing Controller' section describing normalize → dedupe → suppress → budget → escalate pipeline, SST config keys under surfacing:, 7 new Prometheus metric families, and integration touchpoints with internal/intelligence/, internal/scheduler/, internal/notification/decision.go (read-only enrichment input from DecisionEvaluation), internal/telegram/, internal/annotation/ (spec 027 ack/dismiss feedback). scopes.md gained Scope 4 detail with Implementation Plan, Test Plan, Consumer Impact Sweep, and 17-item Definition of Done (all unchecked). scenario-manifest.json gained 4 new entries SCN-021-016..019 with scope=scope-04 status=planned and linkedTests references to future internal/intelligence/surfacing/*_test.go + tests/e2e/surfacing_budget_test.go. Scope 4 left at 'Not Started' per dispatch instructions; a follow-up bubbles.implement dispatch picks up the build. Scopes 1-3 remain Done; controller wraps existing producers without changing their public contracts. Dependencies noted in design: spec 025 (synthesis content quality), spec 054 (notification intelligence decision inputs), spec 027 (annotation feedback signals). RELEASE-MVP:M1a finding addressed; nextRequiredOwner=bubbles.implement when MVP M1a is picked up for build.

Re-close-out after rescope: Scope 4 (Next Smackerel Prioritizer) extracted from spec 021 — its surface (cross-surface surfacing controller spanning digest, Telegram, web push, ntfy, email-out, plus coordination with specs 025 / 027 / 054) is too broad to live as one scope on the intelligence-delivery spec and is owned by its own future spec. Removed: spec.md '## Cross-Surface Surfacing Budget' section, design.md '## Unified Surfacing Controller' section, scopes.md Scope 4 row + full Scope 4 section, scenario-manifest.json entries SCN-021-016..019, the dispatch entry in report.md. State.json: workflowMode reverted from 'improve-existing' to 'full-delivery'; status and certification.status back to 'done'; scopeProgress trimmed back to three certified scopes (Alert Delivery Sweep, Search Logging, Intelligence Health Freshness); reopen metadata cleared. Runtime untouched: controller groundwork already merged at internal/intelligence/surfacing/ remains on trunk as prework for the future prioritizer spec; go test ./internal/intelligence/... ./internal/scheduler/... ./internal/api/... PASS.

Post-sweep drift-guard remediation recert. The scopesdriftguard ratchet test (internal/scopesdriftguard) was failing at 271 broken file references vs ceiling 270 (pre-existing on origin/main, not introduced by the sweep). Root cause for spec 021: the round-12 gaps note documented the never-authored, deferred live-stack e2e test using a backtick code-path form (tests/e2e/alert_delivery_test.go) which the drift guard's path regex counts as a broken file reference. Fix: de-backticked both occurrences in scopes.md to plain prose (the file was never created on disk, so a backtick code-reference was itself the drift); full meaning and the deferred/never-authored explanation preserved. Verification: go test ./internal/scopesdriftguard/... now GREEN (271 -> 270, ceiling 270). No spec.md / design.md / scenario-manifest.json / runtime / test changes; scopes.md prose-only edit. Spec 021 status remains done with no behavior change. certifiedAt bumped to 2026-06-06T13:00:00Z after the edit to satisfy Gate G088.

reconcile-to-doc state reconciliation (bubbles.validate, state-reconciliation owner). Recorded the canonical 'gaps' specialist phase into execution.completedPhaseClaims and certification.certifiedCompletedPhases to clear the Gate G022 bookkeeping drift artifact-lint flagged (1 of 12 required specialist phases missing). No new gaps analysis content invented; this entry records two genuine pre-existing gaps-phase runs already evidenced in report.md: (1) '## Gaps Sweep (2026-04-21, gaps-to-doc R80)' — findings GAP-021-R80-001 (stale report file-location references after engine.go -> alerts.go/alert_producers.go/synthesis.go refactor) and GAP-021-R80-002 (spec '14 days' wording vs implementation 30-day window at lookups.go:79), both fixed, with a 9-row functional-gap analysis concluding 'No Functional Gaps Found'; and (2) '## 2026-06-06 — Sweep Round 12 of 20 (gaps trigger)' (mode gaps-to-doc, sweep-2026-06-06-r20) — findings F1 (G088 post-cert edit), F2/F3/F4 (E2E-API/Stress/Integration DoD evidence-linkage over-claims) all closed inline, committed as git 31e96495 'bubbles(021/sweep-r12-gaps): re-certify after Tier-2 DoD evidence cleanup'. Status remains done; certifiedAt NOT changed (no spec.md/design.md/scopes.md edit); arrays-only reconciliation.

statusDiscipline.scopePickupRule: All dependsOn scopes must be Done. Pick the lowest-numbered eligible scope.
statusDiscipline.specDoneRequires: All scopes done. certification.completedScopes contains all scope IDs and bubbles.validate has certified promotion.

notes: Intelligence delivery wiring feature. Core intelligence engine methods all exist — this feature is about scheduling, producing, and delivering intelligence to the user via existing Telegram infrastructure.
